zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Patoun or Paterson於1565年出生在蘇格蘭金洛-爾金洛斯時，她的父親James George Patoun15歲，母親Margaret Drysdale16歲。她於大約1589年與Andrew Moodie Rankin在蘇格蘭可拉可曼南史爾圖利波迪結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1638年5月22日在英國蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特林享年73歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Patoun or Paterson于1565年出生在苏格兰金洛-爾金洛斯时，她的父亲James George Patoun15岁，母亲Margaret Drysdale16岁。她于大约1589年与Andrew Moodie Rankin在苏格兰可拉可曼南史爾圖利波迪结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1638年5月22日在英国苏格兰斯林史爾斯特林享年73岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Patoun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Patton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Peatáin, see Payton .

English: from either of two places called Patton in Shropshire and Westmorland. The Shropshire placename is recorded as Peattingtune in 901 and derives from an Old English personal name Peatta + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Westmorland placename is recorded as Patun in 1086 and derives from Old English pæth ‘path, track’ or an Old English personal name Patta + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Patton
meanings: English, northern Irish, and Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scottish personal name Patun, Paton, an Old French diminutive of Pat(e), a pet form of Patrick. Compare Patrick and Pattinson .

History: The American general George Patton (1885–1945) was born in San Gabriel, CA, into a family with a long military tradition. His earliest American ancestor, Robert Patton, had emigrated from Scotland to VA c. 1770.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Paterson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Patterson
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of Patrickson ‘son of Patrick ’, which was either shortened to Patrison and metathesized to Patterson, or shortened from Paterickson to Patterson.

Irish: in Ulster, this name is of English or Scottish origin, but in County Galway, it was also taken by bearers of the Gaelic name Ó Caisín ‘descendant of the little curly-headed one’ (from Gaelic casín ‘curly’), which is usually Anglicized as Cussane. In addition to the confusion between Irish Gaelic casín ‘curly’ and cosán ‘path’, there has also been an erroneous assumption that the English name Patterson is somehow derived from the English word path.
English: 19th-century variant of Patteson, a shortened form of Pattinson .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Patoun or Paterson was born in 1565, in Kinross, Kinross-shire, Scotland, her father, James George Patoun, was 15 and her mother, Margaret Drysdale, was 16. She married Andrew Moodie Rankin about 1589, in Tullibody, Clackmannanshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 22 May 1638, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 73, and was buried in Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Patoun or Paterson est née en 1565, à Kinross, Kinross-shire, Écosse, son père, James George Patoun, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Margaret Drysdale, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Andrew Moodie Rankin environ 1589, à Tullibody, Clackmannanshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 22 mai 1638, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Patoun or Paterson 1565 in Kinross, Kinross-shire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater James George Patoun 15 und ihre Mutter Margaret Drysdale 16. Sie hat um 1589 in Tullibody, Clackmannanshire, Scotland Andrew Moodie Rankin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 22. Mai 1638 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Patoun or Paterson è nata il nel 1565 nel Kinross, Kinross-shire, Scotland, sua padre, James George Patoun, aveva 15 anni e sua madre, Margaret Drysdale, aveva 16 anni. Lei sposato Andrew Moodie Rankin circa 1589, nel Tullibody, Clackmannanshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Patoun or Patersonが1565年にスコットランドキンロス-シュアイアキンロスで生まれた時，父James George Patounは15歳，母Margaret Drysdaleは16歳であった。約1589年にスコットランドクラクマナンシュアイアトゥリボディでAndrew Moodie Rankinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1638年5月22日にイギリススコットランドスティルリングシュアイアスティルリングで73歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Patoun or Paterson(이)가 1565년 스컷랜드 킨로스-실르 킨로스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James George Patoun(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Drysdale(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1589년 스컷랜드 클랙먼아실르 투리보디에서 Andrew Moodie Rankin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1638년 5월 22일 영국 스코틀랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Andrew Moodie Rankin aproximadamente 1589, em Tullibody, Clackmannanshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 22 de maio de 1638, em Stirling, Stirlingshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 73 anos, e foi sepultada em Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Patoun or Paterson родилась в 1565 г. в местности кинросс, кинросс-шир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени James George Patoun было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Drysdale, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Andrew Moodie Rankin около 1589 г. в местности туллибоди, клаккманнаньшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 22 мая 1638 г. в местности стирлинг, стирлингшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия, Великобритания.

Murió el 22 de mayo de 1638, en Stirling, Stirlingshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en Escocia, Reino Unido.
